meta.title: Distributed Systems Conference 2025
meta.author: Mauricio Gomez
meta.description: Building a Multi-Model Database That Doesn't Suck — A conference talk

slides.title: Building a Multi-Model Database That Doesn't Suck
slides.subtitle: How we unified 5 data models under a single query engine

slides.title: About me
slides.subtitle: Why I spent 4 years of my life on this problem

items: Previously: core database engineer at a FAANG company (2018–2021)
Built a real-time analytics pipeline handling 2M events/sec
Ran a small SaaS company with 3 databases — hated every minute
Open-source contributor to PostgreSQL, Redis, and ClickHouse
Currently building NebulaDB — open-source, multi-model, single-engine

blocks.content: I wrote the first line of NebulaDB on a flight from SFO to JFK. It didn't compile for 6 months.

slides.title: Every company becomes a database company
slides.subtitle: Whether you like it or not.

items: Stripe: runs Postgres, MongoDB, Elasticsearch, Redis, and FoundationDB
Notion: migrated from Postgres to a custom sharded architecture
Discord: went from MongoDB to Cassandra to ScyllaDB
Figma: built a custom multiplayer sync engine on top of Postgres
Uber: runs 20+ different database systems in production

blocks.content: The average Series B company touches 4.2 different databases. None of them talk to each other.

items: Write to primary DB
Publish change event
Transform and clean
Index in search engine
Invalidate cache
Check cache
Query primary DB
Search index for full-text
Merge results in app code
Handle inconsistencies

slides.title: Single engine, multiple models
slides.subtitle: One storage layer, one query planner, one transaction manager — many access patterns.

items: Storage Engine: LSM-tree with pluggable column families for each model
Query Planner: Model-aware cost-based optimizer — picks the best index per query pattern
Transaction Manager: Serializable snapshot isolation across models, same MVCC layer
Index Layer: B-tree for relational, inverted for text, HNSW for vectors, adjacency list for graph
Wire Protocols: PostgreSQL wire protocol, Redis RESP, HTTP/GraphQL

blocks.content: We use HNSW with M=16, efConstruction=200. 99.2% recall at 10x faster than brute force.

slides.title: What broke (and how we fixed it)

items: LSM compaction storms at 2TB per node → tiered compaction + rate limiting
HNSW graph corruption on crash → write-ahead log for index mutations
Cross-model deadlocks → global wait-for graph with timeouts
Postgres wire protocol edge cases → fuzzed with 10K+ random protocol messages
Memory pressure under mixed workloads → adaptive cache sizing per model

blocks.content: Do not build a multi-model database. Pick one model. Make it fast. Then add the next one. If you try to build everything at once, you will build nothing at all.
